steps.title: Set the current limit
steps.content:
Ohm's law gives
$$I=\frac{V}{R}$$
The voltage is $6n$ and the resistance is $520$, so the current limit is
$$\frac{6n}{520}\le0.25$$

steps.title: Find the battery count
steps.content:
Multiply by $520$ and divide by $6$:
$$6n\le0.25(520)=130$$
$$n\le\frac{130}{6}$$
This limit is $21.666$ and continues, so the greatest whole number of batteries below it is $21$. The answer is 21.
